content:
and we are supposed to interpret your description how? Guess

"Thunderbird suddenly stopped formatting the body of my emails correctly. "

What does that mean? What is correctly? Formatting them when you write them, when you get them or what?

Have you tried repairing the folder if it is an incoming mail issue. (right click, select properties and then the repair button)Sometime indexed get messed up (usually by antivirus programs) but unformatted and often unrelated raw HTML can be the result of indexing issues.

For compose issues try holding the shift key when opening the compose window (that forces text only if using HTML and vise versa and is a diagnostic tool.)
creator: MattAuSupport

content: You didn't provide version of thunderbird. I will assume 140. If 150, just highlight account, rightclick and select 'reset folder order'. Another way, which works in 140, is to use the config editor and set mailnews.default_sort_order to a value of 2.

content:
No need to download Thunderbird again. You just have to find turn the feature on...

At the top of then left-hand Folders Pane, you will see a header bar with three little dots on the right. Clicking that reveals a context menu, one of its options is Folder Modes, and one it its options is Unified Folders.

content:
Found a work around after a bit more searching and having found the link "https://releases.mozilla.org/pub/thunderbird/releases/", scrolled down to the bottom and went into "latest", opened the README.txt and used "https://download.mozilla.org/?product=thunderbird-latest&os=win&lang=en-US" which was relevant to what I was using which actually downloaded the latest version that did not occur using the relevant web page download which was only the previous 150.0 version, ran the installer and then Help/About which again wanted to do another update which I went along with, after which it is now saying it is the current version.

So it looks like a slight tweak is needed on the download web page, but am now happy I got it sorted.
